cues.label: The recurring-extreme cost
cues.headline: Re-scanning is O(n) each time
cues.narration: When you need the smallest again and again as data changes, scanning every time is wasteful.

cues.label: The heap invariant
cues.headline: Parent ≤ children, root is the min
cues.narration: A local promise forces the minimum to the root while leaving the rest loosely ordered.

cues.label: Array with index math
cues.headline: Children at 2i+1 and 2i+2
cues.narration: The tree is stored as a flat array; children and parents are pure index arithmetic — no pointers.

cues.label: Push sifts up, pop sifts down
cues.headline: Repair one root-to-leaf path
cues.narration: Insert drops at the end and sifts up; remove moves the last element to the root and sifts down.

cues.label: The four jobs
cues.headline: Top-k, merge, schedule, running-extreme
cues.narration: Size-k top-k, k-way merge, priority scheduling, and streaming median with two heaps.

cues.label: heapq is min-only
cues.headline: Negate for a max-heap
cues.narration: Python's heapq has no max flag; push negated values and negate on the way out.

cues.label: When it fails
cues.headline: One-shot extreme, full sort, arbitrary search
cues.narration: A single scan beats a heap for a one-shot extreme; heaps do not give full order or middle lookups.
